title: caltha palustris subsp. arctica
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic marsh marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Caltha palustris subsp. arctica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
